testCases.title: Email Validation: Invalid Format (Missing '@')
preconditions: User is on the Registration or Login screen.
steps: Enter 'testexample.com' in the 'Email' field.
Attempt to submit the form or unfocus the field.
testCases.expectedResult: An inline error message is displayed: 'Please enter a valid email address' or similar. Form submission is prevented.

testCases.title: Email Validation: Invalid Format (Missing domain)
preconditions: User is on the Registration or Login screen.
steps: Enter 'test@example' in the 'Email' field.
Attempt to submit the form or unfocus the field.
testCases.expectedResult: An inline error message is displayed: 'Please enter a valid email address' or similar. Form submission is prevented.

testCases.title: Email Validation: Empty Email Field
preconditions: User is on the Registration or Login screen.
steps: Leave the 'Email' field empty.
Attempt to submit the form.
testCases.expectedResult: An inline error message is displayed: 'Email cannot be empty' or 'Email is required'. Form submission is prevented.

testCases.title: Password Complexity: Too Short (assuming min 8 chars)
preconditions: User is on the Registration or Forgot Password (New Password) screen.
Password complexity requires a minimum of 8 characters (assumption).
steps: Enter 'Pass123' (7 characters) in the 'Password' field.
Attempt to submit the form or unfocus the field.
testCases.expectedResult: An inline error message is displayed: 'Password must be at least 8 characters long' or similar. Form submission is prevented.

testCases.title: Password Complexity: Missing Uppercase Character
preconditions: User is on the Registration or Forgot Password (New Password) screen.
Password complexity requires at least one uppercase character (assumption).
steps: Enter 'password123!' in the 'Password' field.
Attempt to submit the form or unfocus the field.
testCases.expectedResult: An inline error message is displayed: 'Password must contain at least one uppercase letter' or similar. Form submission is prevented.

testCases.title: Password Complexity: Missing Number
preconditions: User is on the Registration or Forgot Password (New Password) screen.
Password complexity requires at least one number (assumption).
steps: Enter 'Password!!' in the 'Password' field.
Attempt to submit the form or unfocus the field.
testCases.expectedResult: An inline error message is displayed: 'Password must contain at least one number' or similar. Form submission is prevented.

testCases.title: Password Complexity: Missing Special Character
preconditions: User is on the Registration or Forgot Password (New Password) screen.
Password complexity requires at least one special character (assumption).
steps: Enter 'Password123' in the 'Password' field.
Attempt to submit the form or unfocus the field.
testCases.expectedResult: An inline error message is displayed: 'Password must contain at least one special character' or similar. Form submission is prevented.

testCases.title: Successful Login with Valid Email and Password
preconditions: User has a registered account.
User is on the Login screen.
steps: Enter a registered email address in the 'Email' field.
Enter the correct password for the account in the 'Password' field.
Tap the 'Login' button.
testCases.expectedResult: User is successfully logged in and redirected to the dashboard/home page. Average login time is under 3 seconds.

testCases.title: Attempt Login with Incorrect Password
preconditions: User has a registered account.
User is on the Login screen.
steps: Enter a registered email address in the 'Email' field.
Enter an incorrect password in the 'Password' field.
Tap the 'Login' button.
testCases.expectedResult: An error message is displayed: 'Invalid email or password' or 'Incorrect credentials'.

testCases.title: Attempt Login with Unregistered Email
preconditions: User is on the Login screen.
steps: Enter an email address that is not registered in the 'Email' field.
Enter any password in the 'Password' field.
Tap the 'Login' button.
testCases.expectedResult: An error message is displayed: 'Invalid email or password' or 'Account not found'.

testCases.title: Multiple Failed Login Attempts Leading to Account Lockout
preconditions: User has a registered account.
User is on the Login screen.
System has a defined lockout policy (e.g., 3-5 failed attempts).
steps: Repeatedly enter a registered email and incorrect password (e.g., 5 times).
On the final attempt, enter the correct password.
testCases.expectedResult: After the threshold of incorrect attempts, the account should be locked. A message indicating account lockout or prompting for 'Forgot Password' is displayed. User cannot log in even with correct credentials until lockout period expires or reset.

testCases.title: Login with an Admin-Disabled Account
preconditions: An account exists and has been manually disabled by an administrator.
User is on the Login screen.
steps: Enter the email address of the disabled account.
Enter the correct password.
Tap the 'Login' button.
testCases.expectedResult: An error message is displayed: 'Your account has been disabled. Please contact support.' or similar. User is not logged in.

testCases.title: Network Interruption during Login
preconditions: User is on the Login screen with valid credentials entered.
Device network is active.
steps: Enter valid email and password.
Tap 'Login' button.
Immediately disable network (Wi-Fi/mobile data) on the device before the request completes.
testCases.expectedResult: An error message related to network connectivity is displayed, e.g., 'No internet connection' or 'Network error. Please try again'.

testCases.title: Successful Login via Google SSO (Existing User)
preconditions: User has an existing account linked to a Google ID.
User is on the Login screen.
steps: Tap the 'Sign in with Google' button.
Select the linked Google account from the Google prompt (if multiple accounts).
Authorize the application (if not previously authorized).
testCases.expectedResult: User is successfully logged in and redirected to the dashboard/home page. Average login time is under 3 seconds.

testCases.title: First-time Login via Google SSO (New User - assuming auto-registration)
preconditions: User does not have an existing account in the application.
User has a Google account.
User is on the Login/Registration screen.
steps: Tap the 'Sign in with Google' button.
Select a Google account.
Authorize the application.
testCases.expectedResult: A new user account is automatically created using the Google profile information. User is successfully logged in and redirected to the dashboard/home page, potentially with an onboarding flow.

testCases.title: User Denies Permissions during Google SSO Flow
preconditions: User is on the Login screen.
steps: Tap the 'Sign in with Google' button.
When prompted by Google for permissions, select 'Deny' or 'Cancel'.
testCases.expectedResult: User is returned to the Login screen. A message may be displayed indicating that the login was cancelled or failed due to denied permissions.

testCases.title: Google Service Temporarily Unavailable
preconditions: User is on the Login screen.
Simulate Google SSO service being unavailable (e.g., through a mock/proxy if possible, or observe if Google services are down).
steps: Tap the 'Sign in with Google' button.
testCases.expectedResult: An error message is displayed indicating that Google login is temporarily unavailable, or a general error occurred. User is returned to the Login screen.

testCases.title: Switching Google Accounts during SSO Process
preconditions: User has multiple Google accounts logged into their device.
User is on the Login screen.
steps: Tap the 'Sign in with Google' button.
When prompted, select a Google account that is different from the one that would normally be chosen or the one linked to an existing account.
testCases.expectedResult: If the chosen Google account is linked to an existing app account, the user is logged in. If it's a new Google account, a new app account is created (if auto-registration is enabled). If the Google account is not new and not linked and auto-registration is off, an appropriate error should be shown.

testCases.title: Successful Forgot Password Flow and Password Reset
preconditions: User has a registered account.
User is on the Login screen or Forgot Password initiation screen.
Access to the registered email inbox.
steps: Tap 'Forgot Password?' link.
Enter the registered email address in the 'Email' field.
Tap 'Send Reset Link' or 'Submit' button.
Check email inbox for the password reset link.
Tap the password reset link (opens in browser or app).
Enter a new strong password (meeting complexity) in the 'New Password' field.
Confirm the new password in the 'Confirm New Password' field.
Tap 'Reset Password' or 'Submit' button.
testCases.expectedResult: A confirmation message 'Password reset successful' is displayed. User is redirected to the Login screen. The new password can be used to log in.

testCases.title: Request Password Reset for Unregistered Email
preconditions: User is on the Forgot Password initiation screen.
steps: Enter an email address that is not registered in the 'Email' field.
Tap 'Send Reset Link' or 'Submit' button.
testCases.expectedResult: A message is displayed indicating that if the email is registered, a reset link will be sent (to prevent enumeration attacks). The system should NOT confirm if the email exists.

testCases.title: Using an Expired Password Reset Link
preconditions: A password reset link has been sent to a user.
The link has expired (wait for the configured expiry time).
steps: Attempt to open the expired password reset link.
testCases.expectedResult: An error message is displayed: 'Password reset link has expired. Please request a new one.' User is prompted to request a new link.

testCases.title: Using an Already Used Password Reset Link
preconditions: A password reset link has been sent and successfully used once to reset the password.
steps: Attempt to open the same password reset link again.
testCases.expectedResult: An error message is displayed: 'Password reset link has already been used. Please request a new one.' User is prompted to request a new link.

testCases.title: Setting New Password Not Meeting Complexity Rules During Reset
preconditions: User is on the 'Set New Password' screen via a valid reset link.
steps: Enter a new password that does not meet complexity requirements (e.g., 'short').
Confirm the password.
Tap 'Reset Password' or 'Submit' button.
testCases.expectedResult: An inline error message is displayed detailing the password complexity requirements not met (e.g., 'Password must be at least 8 characters long, contain an uppercase letter, a number, and a special character'). Password is not reset.

testCases.title: Requesting Multiple Password Resets in Quick Succession
preconditions: User has a registered account.
User is on the Forgot Password initiation screen.
steps: Enter the registered email address.
Tap 'Send Reset Link' repeatedly within a short timeframe (e.g., 5 times in 10 seconds).
testCases.expectedResult: The system should apply rate limiting, allowing only one reset link request within a certain period (e.g., 60 seconds). Subsequent requests should yield a message like 'You can only request a password reset once every X minutes' or similar.

testCases.title: Network Interruption during Password Reset Link Generation
preconditions: User is on the Forgot Password initiation screen with valid email.
Device network is active.
steps: Enter valid email address.
Tap 'Send Reset Link' button.
Immediately disable network (Wi-Fi/mobile data) on the device before the request completes.
testCases.expectedResult: An error message related to network connectivity is displayed, e.g., 'No internet connection' or 'Network error. Please try again'.

testCases.title: Successful User Registration with Valid Credentials
preconditions: User is on the Registration screen.
steps: Enter a unique, valid email address in the 'Email' field.
Enter a strong, valid password (meeting complexity requirements) in the 'Password' field.
Confirm the password in the 'Confirm Password' field.
Tap the 'Register' or 'Sign Up' button.
testCases.expectedResult: User account is successfully created, and the user is redirected to the dashboard/home page or presented with a 'Registration Successful' message. A confirmation email may be sent.

testCases.title: Attempt to Register with an Already Existing Email
preconditions: An account with a specific email address already exists.
steps: Enter an email address that is already registered in the 'Email' field.
Enter a valid password.
Confirm the password.
Tap the 'Register' or 'Sign Up' button.
testCases.expectedResult: An error message is displayed indicating that the email address is already registered, e.g., 'This email is already in use' or 'Account already exists'.

testCases.title: Attempt to Register without Confirming Password
preconditions: User is on the Registration screen.
steps: Enter a valid email address.
Enter a valid password in the 'Password' field.
Leave the 'Confirm Password' field empty.
Tap the 'Register' or 'Sign Up' button.
testCases.expectedResult: An error message is displayed, e.g., 'Please confirm your password' or 'Passwords do not match'.

testCases.title: Attempt to Register with Mismatched Passwords
preconditions: User is on the Registration screen.
steps: Enter a valid email address.
Enter a valid password in the 'Password' field (e.g., 'Password123!').
Enter a different password in the 'Confirm Password' field (e.g., 'Password123').
Tap the 'Register' or 'Sign Up' button.
testCases.expectedResult: An error message is displayed indicating that the passwords do not match, e.g., 'Passwords do not match'.

testCases.title: Network Interruption during Registration
preconditions: User is on the Registration screen with valid input.
Device network is active.
steps: Enter valid email and password.
Tap 'Register' or 'Sign Up' button.
Immediately disable network (Wi-Fi/mobile data) on the device before the request completes.
testCases.expectedResult: An error message related to network connectivity is displayed, e.g., 'No internet connection' or 'Network error. Please try again'.
